articles.title: 'AI isn't just..': Anand Mahindra backs Palantir CEO's theory, sees India's edge lie elsewhere
articles.summary: Drawing from his recent shareholder letter to Tech Mahindra, Mahindra compared today's AI landscape to the smartphone revolution. "A smartphone is remarkable technology," he noted, "but it became indispensable because of the apps and experiences built on top of it."

articles.title: Bengaluru developer bought old bank hardware for ₹3.5 Lakh. What it earns him now is stunning
articles.summary: A viral post claims a 27-year-old Bengaluru developer transformed decommissioned bank servers bought for ₹3.5 lakh at a government auction into an AI setup that now reportedly earns him ₹27 lakh a month.

articles.title: US stock market today: Wall street trades mixed after job data release; Dow jumps 400 points, Nasdaq trades flat
articles.summary: Wall Street saw a mixed trading session as a weaker-than-expected US jobs report fueled hopes the Federal Reserve might pause interest rate hikes. The Dow and S&P 500 edged up, while the Nasdaq dipped.

articles.title: LPG consumption falls 8% in H1 2026 as West Asia conflict disrupts supplies
articles.summary: According to provisional data from the Petroleum Planning and Analysis Cell (PPAC), India's LPG consumption stood at around 14.74 million tonnes (mt) during January-June 2026, compared with 15.95 mt in the same period last year.

articles.title: Japan’s Rs 1 trillion investment push in India: PM Modi and Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi unveil initiatives to boost ties
articles.summary: Highlighting the growing economic partnership between the two nations, PM Modi said more than 100 new business agreements had been signed over the past year, paving the way for Japanese investments worth over $10 billion in India.

articles.title: US adds just 57,000 jobs in June as hiring loses momentum despite easing unemployment
articles.summary: US job creation significantly decelerated in June, with only 57,000 new positions added, a stark drop from May's revised figure. While the unemployment rate dipped slightly to 4.2%, the labor force participation rate also declined.

articles.title: Japan bets ₹1 lakh crore on India: Manufacturing, AI and semiconductors lead 120 new India-Japan deals
articles.summary: Japan is ramping up its economic engagement with India, committing nearly ₹1 trillion in investments across manufacturing, semiconductors, AI, green energy and finance.

articles.title: Market wrap: Top gainers and losers on NSE Nifty50 and BSE Sensex today
articles.summary: Indian stock markets surged on Thursday, with Sensex and Nifty climbing nearly 1%. A significant rally in IT stocks, coupled with easing crude oil prices and positive India-Japan strategic agreements, boosted investor confidence.

articles.title: Samsung Galaxy Glasses leak: 12MP camera, Gemini AI, reads your notifications aloud — full feature breakdown
articles.summary: Samsung's upcoming Galaxy Glasses may bring Gemini AI, a 12MP camera, notification readouts, Galaxy Ring gesture controls, and deep Android XR integration to wearable tech.

articles.title: 'Digital Pizza only ': UK Domino's has a hilarious response to PlayStation ending Physical game discs
articles.summary: Domino's also mocked its famous slogan.

articles.title: Flying with sports gear? Air India cuts a major travel cost, Check if your ticket qualifies
articles.summary: Air India has waived off handling charges for sports equipment across select fare families on both domestic and international routes, making it more convenient for customers travelling with their sporting gear.

articles.title: 'If crude remains at current level...': Hardeep Puri on when petrol, diesel prices may come down
articles.summary: Petrol and diesel being processed currently are bought at higher prices, says Hardeep Singh Puri

articles.title: Air India Express restores Middle East flights: Check routes, destinations and schedules
articles.summary: Air India Express has fully reinstated its Middle East flight operations, bringing back services to Kuwait and Salalah. Flights to Kuwait from Kozhikode and Bengaluru are resuming with increased frequencies.

articles.title: Tata Sierra EV vs Harrier EV vs Curvv EV: Which electric SUV should you actually buy in 2026?
articles.summary: Tata's three electric SUVs target different buyers: the Curvv EV offers value, the Harrier EV prioritizes space, and the new Sierra EV leads with the longest range, quickest acceleration, and most advanced features.

articles.title: Messi and Mbappe both on 6 goals: The FIFA World Cup 2026 Golden Boot race is now a thriller with 5 contenders
articles.summary: Lionel Messi and Kylian Mbappe are tied on 6 goals in the FIFA World Cup 2026 Golden Boot race, with Erling Haaland, Vinicius Jr and Ousmane Dembele still in contention. The battle for football's biggest individual prize is just heating up.

articles.title: FPIs continue to exit India: June equity outflows hit Rs 49,340 crore as debt inflows persist
articles.summary: Foreign investors continued their exit from Indian equities in June, withdrawing Rs 49,340 crore amid global risk aversion and domestic valuation concerns. Despite a brief February recovery, outflows have reached Rs 2.7 lakh crore this year, surpassing 2025's total.

articles.title: 2017 Telugu box office disaster becomes the first film in the world to cross 1 billion YouTube views — the full story
articles.summary: Jaya Janaki Nayaka, the 2017 Telugu flop dubbed as Khoonkar in Hindi, has become the first film in the world to cross 1 billion YouTube views.

articles.title: Did you know? Why fuel tank arrows exist on your dashboard
articles.summary: That small arrow next to your fuel gauge points to your fuel cap side. Invented by Ford designer Jim Moylan in 1986, here's the surprisingly clever story behind this tiny detail.

articles.title: China's viral rooftop mist is cooling entire neighbourhoods. Here's the science behind it
articles.summary: China has experienced more frequent and longer-lasting heatwaves in recent years, a trend that scientists link to climate change.

articles.title: Japanese PM Takaichi Lands in India — Northeast Emerges as Strategic Pivot
articles.summary: Japanese PM Takaichi visits India, highlighting northeast as a strategic hub. Talks with PM Modi focus on Act East Policy, connectivity, and Indo-Pacific regional security.

articles.title: Digestive Boost: Small Habits, Big Impact — Gut Reset Guide Merges Fermented Foods with Safer Cooking
articles.summary: Boost gut health with a week of fermented foods, beta-glucans, and safer cookware habits. Simple, high-impact lifestyle tweaks enhance digestion, immunity, and metabolic wellness.

articles.title: Best bikes under ₹2 lakh in India 2026: Royal Enfield, Yamaha, Bajaj and more compared
articles.summary: From Royal Enfield Hunter 350 to Yamaha R15 V4, here are India's 5 best motorcycles under ₹2 lakh in 2026 — with prices, features and performance compared for every rider type.

articles.title: This 'worn gear' package hid gold worth ₹21.4 crore: Here's how smuggling racket worked
articles.summary: The operation began after DRI officers intercepted an international courier consignment arriving from Thailand at the Courier Terminal in Delhi

articles.title: iPhone Ultra vs Galaxy Z Fold 8 wide: Display, camera and battery compared in detail
articles.summary: Apple's iPhone Ultra and Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide are set to define 2026's foldable phone battle. Here's how they compare on display, camera, chipset and battery life.

articles.title: America turns 250—and Indian-origin CEOs are powering its biggest companies
articles.summary: Indian-origin leaders are reshaping global technology, finance and consumer business, from Microsoft and Google to the World Bank, IBM, Adobe, YouTube and P&G.

articles.title: Honda Activa E: Price, range, top speed and every spec you need to know before buying
articles.summary: Honda Activa e starts at ₹1.20 lakh with 102 km range, 80 km/h top speed, swappable batteries and RoadSync connectivity. Here's everything you need to know before buying.

articles.title: India-Japan trade: PM Modi, 'younger sister' Takaichi sign MoC including $10 billion investment, AI boost
articles.summary: India and Japan elevated their trade and strategic ties with a new Memorandum of Cooperation signed during the 16th Annual Summit in New Delhi. Prime Minister Modi welcomed his Japanese counterpart, highlighting shared priorities for a free and prosperous Indo-Pacific.

articles.title: Who should file ITR & is income tax return filing mandatory for salaried taxpayers?
articles.summary: The important thing to understand is that ITR filing is a statutory requirement and an important financial record-keeping.

articles.title: India's EV boom could save Rs 1 lakh crore in oil imports by 2030: SBI
articles.summary: India stands to save a staggering Rs 1 lakh crore by 2030 if electric vehicles capture 20% of the market, a recent SBI report reveals. The surge in EV adoption, accelerated by geopolitical events, saw registrations jump significantly.

articles.title: OpenAI weighs giving Trump administration 5% stake amid US scrutiny of AI firms: Report
articles.summary: OpenAI reportedly explored offering the Trump administration a 5% stake, a move aimed at fostering government ties and addressing public profit-sharing concerns amid rising AI scrutiny.

articles.title: New EPF Scheme 2026: What it means for your provident fund savings
articles.summary: India’s provident fund framework has now entered a new phase with the notification of the Employees’ Provident Funds Scheme, 2026 (EPF Scheme, 2026) under the Code on Social Security, 2020.

articles.title: Top stocks to buy today: Stock recommendations for July 2, 2026 - check list
articles.summary: Top stock market recommendations: REC, Oberoi Realty, and Home First Finance Company India have been recommended by Aakash K Hindocha, Vice President - Research, Nuvama Professional Clients Group/Nuvama Wealth Management as top stocks to buy on July 2, 2026.

articles.title: Stock Market Live Updates Today: BSE Sensex opens over 250 points up, Nifty50 above 24,050; crude oil prices continue to drop
Unable to withdraw PF or file claims? EPFO services unavailable for 7 days
articles.summary: Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) services remain unavailable beyond the scheduled July 1 deadline due to a planned database consolidation and software upgrade.

articles.title: UPI volumes up 1.2%, rise to 76 crore
articles.summary: Digital payment volumes saw a slight dip in June, with UPI transactions falling 2.1% month-on-month. Despite this, daily UPI activity remained robust, and year-on-year growth continues to impress.

articles.title: Car sales in June signal strong demand recovery
articles.summary: India's passenger vehicle sector kicked off FY27 with robust June sales, fueled by strong demand for SUVs and EVs. Tata Motors saw a remarkable 69% surge, driven by popular models and record EV figures.

articles.title: FPIs' $5bn bond purchase offsets steady equity sales
articles.summary: Foreign investors returned as net buyers in June, injecting $531 million into Indian markets after a period of outflows. This positive shift was driven by substantial inflows into sovereign debt, totaling $5.8 billion, which more than compensated for continued equity selling.

articles.title: PE-VC deal value dips 5% in H1 CY2026
articles.summary: Private equity and venture capital investments saw a slight dip of 5% year-on-year in the first half of 2026, reaching $17.5 billion. Despite global uncertainties, investors deployed substantial capital, particularly in data centers and NBFCs.

articles.title: Hero Moto to invest 3,200 crore in Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: Hero MotoCorp is injecting Rs 3,200 crore into Andhra Pradesh over the next three to five years. This significant investment will bolster manufacturing, enhance its global supply chain, and expand into new business areas.

articles.title: Brands get quick-comm boost in summer sales
articles.summary: Scorching heatwaves have significantly boosted sales for consumer brands, with quick-commerce platforms witnessing a surge in demand for ice creams, beverages, and sunscreens. Ice cream sales alone soared by 140% year-on-year in May.
